entries.name: event-stream
entries.description: Maintainer account compromised; malicious flatmap-stream dependency added to steal cryptocurrency wallets from copay app
indicators: flatmap-stream dependency

entries.name: ua-parser-js
entries.description: Maintainer's npm account hijacked; malicious versions installed cryptominer and password stealer
indicators: jsextension binary
preinstall script

entries.name: colors
entries.description: Maintainer published versions with infinite loop that prints non-ASCII characters; affected Node.js applications globally
indicators: LIBERTY LIBERTY LIBERTY
infinite loop in colors.js

entries.name: faker
entries.description: Maintainer wiped repo and published empty version, breaking every downstream user
indicators: empty package, README only

entries.name: node-ipc
entries.description: Maintainer added code that overwrites files on systems with Russian/Belarusian IPs
indicators: peacenotwar
ssl-geospec dependency

entries.name: coa
entries.description: Maintainer account hijacked; malicious versions installed a Windows credential stealer (DanaBot). Auto-installed by react-scripts users.
indicators: compile.js running curl/wget
DanaBot trojan installer

entries.name: rc
entries.description: Maintainer account hijacked alongside coa; same DanaBot installer payload pushed to rc users (16M+ weekly downloads).
indicators: compile.js postinstall
DanaBot trojan installer

entries.name: eslint-scope
entries.description: Maintainer credentials stolen via reused password; attacker published a backdoored version that exfiltrated npm credentials of any user who installed it. Transitive dependency of eslint-config-airbnb-base, babel-eslint, webpack.
indicators: build/eslint-scope.js posts ~/.npmrc to pastebin

entries.name: @pnpm/exe
entries.description: Class of pnpm-namespace squats and lookalikes (e.g., `pnpm-exe`, `@p-npm/exe`, `pnp-m`) targeting users installing pnpm or its companion binaries via npm; impostor packages drop curl-based loaders.
indicators: @pnpm/exe lookalike namespace
postinstall curl|bash loader

entries.name: polyfill.io
entries.description: polyfill.io domain sold to Funnull; CDN began injecting mobile-specific redirects to sports-betting and scam pages on ~100k sites including JSTOR, Intuit, World Economic Forum. Anyone who left `<script src="https://cdn.polyfill.io/...">` in production was affected.
indicators: <script src="https://cdn.polyfill.io"
redirects to googie-anaiytics[.]com

entries.name: left-pad
entries.description: Maintainer Azer Koculu unpublished left-pad after a dispute with kik over the kik package name. Removal broke thousands of builds including Babel and React. npm subsequently restored the package and changed unpublish policy. This is the foundational npm supply-chain trust event.
indicators: maintainer unpublish
npm trust event

entries.name: crossenv
entries.description: Typosquat of cross-env that ran a postinstall script exfiltrating environment variables to npm.hofmannsven.com. Part of a 38-package typosquat wave by user `hacktask` that triggered the first wave of npm-side anti-typosquat tooling.
indicators: postinstall script
env var exfil
hacktask

entries.name: babelcli
entries.description: Typosquat of babel-cli; part of the August 2017 `hacktask` typosquat wave that also published crossenv, mongose, d3.js, http-proxy.js etc., each carrying an env-variable exfiltration payload.
indicators: hacktask wave
postinstall exfil

entries.name: http-proxy.js
entries.description: Typosquat of http-proxy that exfiltrated environment variables on install. Part of the August 2017 hacktask wave (38 packages).
indicators: hacktask wave

entries.name: mongose
entries.description: Typosquat of mongoose ORM that ran the same env-exfil postinstall payload as crossenv. Part of the August 2017 hacktask wave.
indicators: hacktask wave

entries.name: d3.js
entries.description: Typosquat of d3 that exfiltrated env via postinstall script. Part of August 2017 hacktask wave; one of the first widely-publicized npm typosquats.
indicators: hacktask wave

entries.name: getcookies
entries.description: Express middleware that added a hidden HTTP-header-activated reverse shell. Reached production via mailparser dependency chain. npm removed the package within 24 hours of report.
indicators: mailparser dependency
hidden HTTP header reverse shell

entries.name: flatmap-stream
entries.description: The actual malicious payload injected into event-stream@3.3.6 — a flatmap utility whose minified-and-encrypted release block targeted the Copay bitcoin wallet's electron build, exfiltrating private keys.
indicators: copay wallet targeting
event-stream dependency

entries.name: electron-native-notify
entries.description: First major npm supply-chain attack targeting Electron apps. Compromised package injected into komodo-related wallet builds; exfiltrated wallet seeds to attacker C2.
indicators: Komodo wallet
Agama wallet

entries.name: bb-builder
entries.description: Part of the same Komodo Agama wallet supply-chain attack; bb-builder pulled electron-native-notify in as a dependency, indirectly delivering the wallet stealer to downstream wallet builds.
indicators: Komodo Agama

entries.name: twilio-npm
entries.description: Typosquat of twilio that delivered a Discord token stealer on install. Removed within 48h of disclosure.
indicators: Discord token theft

entries.name: discord.js-user
entries.description: Discord token grabber targeting Discord.js community. Stole user tokens via Electron LocalStorage access and exfiltrated to a Discord webhook controlled by the attacker.
indicators: Discord token theft
Electron LocalStorage

entries.name: fallguys
entries.description: Capitalized on Fall Guys game hype to publish a fake API client that ran a Discord token stealer + clipboard hijacker on install.
indicators: Fall Guys hype
Discord token theft

entries.name: @ledgerhq/connect-kit
entries.description: Maintainer phishing led to a compromised publish that swapped the real connect-kit module for a wallet drainer in dApps that loaded it via CDN. Affected Sushi, Lido, Zapper, RevokeCash and others; estimated $600k+ stolen before remediation.
indicators: wallet drainer
phishing of maintainer
Web3 dApp injection

entries.name: lottie-player
entries.description: @lottiefiles/lottie-player publish token stolen; malicious versions inserted a wallet-drainer popup on every site that loaded the player from CDN.
indicators: CDN drainer
publish token theft

entries.name: @solana/web3.js
entries.description: Maintainer publish token compromised. Affected versions exfiltrated private keys from any Node.js process that initialized a Solana keypair, posting them to a hardcoded gateway.
indicators: private key exfil
publish token theft

entries.name: everything
entries.description: Author published a package whose only purpose was to depend on every other npm package, exhausting registry / proxy caches and effectively griefing the ecosystem. npm has since tightened publish quotas.
indicators: dependency explosion

entries.name: rand-user-agent
entries.description: Maintainer credentials phished. Malicious versions of @wdio/rand-user-agent shipped a Python-based RAT plus an XMR cryptominer that auto-launched on import in Node.js test runners.
indicators: Python RAT
XMR miner

entries.name: es5-ext
entries.description: Maintainer added a postinstall war-protest banner that printed an anti-war message on systems with Russian locale. Triggered downstream alerts as a behavior change in a low-level utility used by ESLint and many others.
indicators: protestware
Russian-locale targeting

entries.name: pdf-to-office
entries.description: Newly-published package whose `prepare` script downloaded and ran a reverse shell binary from a public storage URL on install.
indicators: postinstall reverse shell

entries.name: noblox.js-server
entries.description: Typosquat campaign against the Roblox-developer-focused noblox.js library. Multiple impostor packages (noblox.js-server, noblox.js-vps, noblox.js-vpn) installed Discord token stealers and RATs targeting Roblox developers.
indicators: Roblox developer targeting
Discord token theft

entries.name: ethers-provider2
entries.description: Typosquat of @ethersproject/providers / ethers; targeted Web3 developers with a wallet drainer that ran on `npm install` and on every subsequent `npm run` invocation through a postinstall hook.
indicators: Web3 drainer
postinstall persistence

entries.name: backslash
entries.description: Compromised version 0.2.1 from the Sept 8 2025 npm maintainer-account takeover of 'Qix' (Josh Junon), who was phished by a fake 2FA-reset email from npmjs.help. The attacker published malicious versions of ~24 widely-used packages (collectively billions of weekly downloads) carrying a browser-targeting crypto-drainer that hooks web APIs to silently rewrite cryptocurrency wallet addresses/transactions; largely inert in pure Node.js/server contexts. Live ~2.5h before npm removed them. ONLY this version is malicious — other versions are clean.
indicators: only version 0.2.1 is malicious (pin away from it)
maintainer 'Qix' phished via fake 2FA reset from npmjs.help
browser crypto-drainer rewrites wallet addresses by hooking web APIs
inert server-side; activates in browser bundles

entries.name: node-ipc
entries.description: Second, unrelated node-ipc compromise (2026-05-14, distinct from the 2022 'peacenotwar' protestware): three releases shipped an obfuscated credential stealer that runs on require("node-ipc") (CommonJS entrypoint load, not an npm lifecycle script, making it stealthy for consumers). It collects environment variables, host info, /etc/hosts and a broad set of developer/cloud/package-manager/SCM/Kubernetes/database/SSH credential files, then gzip+base64-encodes, XORs with a SHA-256-derived keystream, and exfiltrates over DNS TXT queries to sh.azurestaticprovider.net (suffix bt.node.js).
indicators: malicious versions 9.1.6, 9.2.3, 12.0.1 (2026 incident)
payload runs on require("node-ipc"), not via lifecycle scripts
DNS-TXT C2 to sh.azurestaticprovider.net, suffix bt.node.js
gzip+base64+XOR(SHA-256 keystream) of stolen credential files

entries.name: async-pipeline-builder
entries.description: Part of the 'TrapDoor' supply-chain campaign (Socket, from 2026-05-22) — the first to weaponize npm, PyPI and crates.io simultaneously, disguising a crypto-focused credential stealer as developer/security tools aimed at crypto/DeFi/Solana/AI developers. Steals SSH keys, Sui/Solana/Aptos wallet keystores, AWS credentials, GitHub tokens, browser profile + wallet-extension data, env vars and API keys. npm execution path: a postinstall hook on install.
indicators: part of the multi-ecosystem 'TrapDoor' campaign (npm+PyPI+crates, from 2026-05-22)
disguised as a crypto/DeFi/web3 security or dev tool
malicious postinstall lifecycle hook
exfiltrates SSH keys, crypto wallet keystores, AWS/GitHub creds, browser data

entries.description: Part of the 'TrapDoor' supply-chain campaign (Socket, from 2026-05-22) — the first to weaponize npm, PyPI and crates.io simultaneously, disguising a crypto-focused credential stealer as developer/security tools aimed at crypto/DeFi/Solana/AI developers. Steals SSH keys, Sui/Solana/Aptos wallet keystores, AWS credentials, GitHub tokens, browser profile + wallet-extension data, env vars and API keys. npm execution path: a postinstall hook on install.
indicators: part of the multi-ecosystem 'TrapDoor' campaign (npm+PyPI+crates, from 2026-05-22)
disguised as a crypto/DeFi/web3 security or dev tool
malicious postinstall lifecycle hook
exfiltrates SSH keys, crypto wallet keystores, AWS/GitHub creds, browser data

entries.name: atomic-lockfile
entries.description: Malicious npm package installed by the 'Atomic Arch' campaign (Sonatype, 2026-06-11), which hijacked ~1,500 orphaned Arch User Repository (AUR) projects and modified their PKGBUILDs to install this package at build time. Rootkit-like credential harvester targeting SSH artifacts, HashiCorp Vault tokens, browser cookies and collaboration-app data stores.
indicators: installed via hijacked AUR PKGBUILD (Atomic Arch campaign)
harvests SSH artifacts, HashiCorp Vault tokens, browser cookies
rootkit-like credential/secret exfiltration

entries.name: @automagik/genie
entries.description: Compromised in the self-propagating 'CanisterWorm' npm worm (TeamPCP, Socket/JFrog 2026-04-22, downstream of the Trivy CI/CD breach). The malicious versions harvest credentials — env-var secrets/tokens, SSH keys, .npmrc, AWS/Azure/GCP creds, and Chrome/MetaMask/Phantom wallet data — and exfiltrate to a webhook plus an Internet Computer (ICP) canister endpoint (telemetry.api-monitor.com, canister cjn37-uyaaa-aaaac-qgnva-cai). It self-spreads by stealing the victim's npm token, republishing packages they can publish with a new malicious postinstall hook (with PyPI-propagation logic too). Only the listed versions are malicious.
indicators: compromised via the CanisterWorm self-propagating npm worm (TeamPCP)
harvests env secrets, SSH keys, .npmrc, cloud creds, MetaMask/Phantom wallets
exfiltrates to telemetry.api-monitor.com + ICP canister cjn37-uyaaa-aaaac-qgnva-cai
steals npm token and republishes victim packages with a malicious postinstall
only the listed versions are malicious

entries.name: @solana-labs/ancor
entries.description: Part of the JFrog-disclosed 'Solana FakeFix' campaign (2026-06-11): ~25 npm/PyPI packages impersonating Solana developer tooling (@solana/web3.js, @solana/spl-token) and luring developers with fake 'stable/fixed/patched/LTS' builds. On install/import it steals Solana keypairs (~/.config/solana/id.json), SSH keys (~/.ssh/id_rsa), AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials), source-control tokens, and .env secrets (vars named KEY/SECRET/MNEMONIC/TOKEN).
indicators: Solana FakeFix campaign (JFrog, 2026-06-11)
impersonates @solana/web3.js or @solana/spl-token
reads ~/.config/solana/id.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.aws/credentials, .env
exfiltrates Solana keypairs, SSH/AWS/SCM tokens
